title: Delicate Crumbly Korean Citrus Anju
description: A citrusy and crumbly Korean dish featuring white vinegar, traditionally cooked in ttukbaegi earthenware, and wrapped in a corn husk, perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: Preheat the ttukbaegi earthenware or a small ceramic bowl over low heat.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, and salt.
Gradually add in the white vinegar, citrus juice, and water, stirring until a crumbly dough forms.
Add the sesame oil and sugar to the dough and stir until just combined.
Knead the dough gently for about 5 minutes until it becomes cohesive and crumbly.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and shape each into a small ball.
Wrap each ball in a corn husk, making sure to seal the edges tightly.
Place the wrapped anju in the preheated ttukbaegi or ceramic bowl and cook for 10-15 minutes on each side, or until lightly browned and crispy.
Serve the anju warm and enjoy.
